lane-selection.evidence: Every estate code repository must default automatic events to Velnor, run weekly schedules on both lanes, and expose github/both through the canonical ci.yml matrix.

checkout.evidence: Every estate code repository consumes checked-out source in CI.

tool-setup.evidence: Estate law requires committed mise configuration and the pinned mise action for CI tools.

rust-ci.evidence: All 13 repositories contain Rust source or a Rust CI/build helper requiring canonical validation.

integration-services.evidence: No independently required service-backed integration concern is present unless overridden by repository product evidence.

cargo-cache.evidence: Every estate repository compiles Rust code or Rust automation and therefore requires the standard local sccache plus Cargo registry cache contract.

docker-build.evidence: No image product/build concern is present unless overridden by repository product evidence.

artifacts.evidence: No cross-job or published artifact concern is present unless overridden by repository workflow evidence.

docs-pages.evidence: No documentation build or Pages surface is present unless overridden by repository workflow evidence.

preview.evidence: No preview product surface is present unless overridden by repository workflow evidence.

release.evidence: No repository-local publish/tag workflow is present unless overridden by repository workflow evidence.

renovate.evidence: Renovate is externally hosted unless a repository-local execution workflow is recorded.

required-aggregator.evidence: Every estate CI surface exposes one stable lane-suffixed aggregate result for branch protection; requiring selected leaf jobs can silently omit later jobs.

workflow-safety.evidence: Every executable estate workflow requires bounded concurrency and job timeout safety; ci.yml is the universal baseline.

rust-ci.evidence: The production monorepo uses path-classified per-package Rust jobs and an aggregate required check; package identities are explicit repository parameters under the approved Class-A L2/L3 model.

checkout.evidence: Every source-consuming Class-A workload job must use the canonical checkout pin and credential boundary.

tool-setup.evidence: Every Class-A Rust workload job installs its declared tools through the canonical mise action.

cargo-cache.evidence: Every compiling Class-A workload job uses the same local compiler and Cargo registry cache contract.

integration-services.evidence: Ansible and Kestra workflows exercise product-specific integration surfaces; their commands and services remain explicit repository parameters.

docker-build.evidence: rust-docker.yml and kestra-build-publish.yml build product images.

artifacts.evidence: Package and image fan-in is tied to the monorepo build graph.

release.evidence: Image publication is coupled to product build workflows rather than a generic tag release.

rust-ci.evidence: Rust-script is the Docker factory control plane; build-publish sweep validates it instead of a library test job.

checkout.evidence: Every Docker-factory workload consumes repository source through canonical checkout without persisted credentials.

tool-setup.evidence: Every Docker-factory workload installs rust-script through canonical mise setup.

cargo-cache.evidence: The Rust-script control plane caches compiled scripts by an explicit source hash; product compilation occurs in registry-cached BuildKit layers rather than Cargo CI jobs.

docker-build.evidence: The repository product is a multi-architecture blockchain image factory.

artifacts.evidence: Images publish directly to the registry; no Results Service artifact fan-in is used.

release.evidence: Image publication occurs in build-publish.yml with a single-writer lane rather than tag assets.

checkout.evidence: Jackin's canonical telemetry conformance compile job consumes repository source through pinned checkout.

tool-setup.evidence: Jackin's canonical telemetry conformance compile job installs its locked Rust toolchain through mise.

rust-ci.evidence: Jackin splits validation into reusable workflows; telemetry-conformance is the canonical directly declared Rust compile job.

cargo-cache.evidence: The canonical Jackin compile job uses local-only sccache and the repository's native Cargo-registry cache adapter.

integration-services.evidence: Construct, capsule, and exact-result graphs are Jackin product-specific orchestration.

docker-build.evidence: construct.yml builds and publishes the Jackin image.

artifacts.evidence: Construct transports platform digests into the single-writer manifest job.

docs-pages.evidence: docs.yml builds and deploys the documentation site.

preview.evidence: preview.yml produces preview artifacts and attestations.

checkout.evidence: The canonical Parallax check job consumes the workspace through pinned checkout without persisted credentials.

tool-setup.evidence: The canonical Parallax check job installs Rust through the pinned mise action.

rust-ci.evidence: Parallax splits validation by concern; check is the canonical representative Rust compile job.

cargo-cache.evidence: The canonical Parallax check job uses lane-scoped Cargo registry storage and local-only sccache.

integration-services.evidence: storage-integration.yml and scheduled-measurement.yml exercise Parallax-specific telemetry/storage systems.

docker-build.evidence: Parallax publishes native archives and preview artifacts; no workflow builds or publishes an OCI image.

artifacts.evidence: CI, preview, and release transport binaries and reports between jobs.

preview.evidence: preview.yml is the product preview workflow.

integration-services.evidence: The playground itself is a polyglot telemetry integration sample; its Java/Rust commands are repository parameters.
Native Swift/FFI parity is a TableRock product concern represented by native.yml; it is an all-macOS application gate and therefore cannot use the Linux Velnor lane matrix.

artifacts.evidence: Generated Swift bindings and native evidence are repository-specific outputs.

release.evidence: native-release.yml builds, Developer ID signs, notarizes, staples, and archives the macOS application; this Apple-only product release cannot use the Linux Velnor lane matrix.

rust-ci.evidence: Both primary tests and integration clippy compile Rust and must use the identical canonical setup stack.

cargo-cache.evidence: Every Holla compiling job uses the canonical lane-scoped local compiler and Cargo registry cache contract.

artifacts.evidence: Release workflows produce Debian and cross-built binary artifacts.

release.evidence: release.yml and release-deb.yml publish distinct approved release products.

rust-ci.evidence: Velnor test and clippy jobs compile the workspace and must use the identical canonical setup stack.

cargo-cache.evidence: Every Velnor compiling job uses the canonical lane-scoped local compiler and Cargo registry cache contract.

integration-services.evidence: Runner protocol, fixture, and daemon integration tests are Velnor-specific.

docker-build.evidence: Velnor releases native runner archives and Debian packages; the canonical job-image Dockerfile is packaged and validated but no repository workflow builds or publishes an OCI image.

artifacts.evidence: Release workflows transport Debian packages and release evidence.

release.evidence: release.yml and release-deb.yml publish Velnor releases through the signed apt path.

rust-ci.evidence: Primary tests, clippy, and agent cross-build all compile Rust and use the canonical setup stack.

cargo-cache.evidence: Every Ruxel compiling job uses the canonical lane-scoped local compiler and Cargo registry cache contract.

integration-services.evidence: Ansible and uv oracle checks are Ruxel product-specific commands inside ci.yml.

release.evidence: No repository-local release workflow exists on the delivered surface.

rust-ci.evidence: The primary, latest-toolchain, crossterm, and semver jobs comprise Termrock's Rust compile graph.

cargo-cache.evidence: Every Termrock compiling job uses the canonical lane-scoped local compiler and Cargo registry cache contract.

docs-pages.evidence: docs.yml builds and deploys the Termrock documentation site.

artifacts.evidence: Documentation and release workflows transport generated outputs.

rust-ci.evidence: Library validation and PostgreSQL integration both compile Rust through the canonical setup stack.

cargo-cache.evidence: Every Schemalane compiling job uses the canonical lane-scoped local compiler and Cargo registry cache contract.

integration-services.evidence: Schemalane's database contract requires PostgreSQL integration coverage; the canonical integration job uses repository-parameterized testcontainers rather than a YAML service declaration.
PostgreSQL compatibility tests are encoded as repository commands in canonical ci.yml without a separately managed service concern.
